    90    317.9 MiB    317.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    317.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    317.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    318.2 MiB    318.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value > target:
   113    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    318.6 MiB    318.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(

Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
    90    318.6 MiB    318.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    317.9 MiB    317.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    318.5 MiB      0.6 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    317.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    318.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    318.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    318.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    318.6 MiB    318.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   115    318.5 MiB    318.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    318.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    318.8 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    318.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents

   133    318.4 MiB    318.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    318.8 MiB      0.4 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    318.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    318.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    318.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    318.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    318.6 MiB    318.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    319.0 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    318.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    319.0 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    319.0 MiB    319.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    319.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    319.5 MiB      0.4 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    319.5 MiB    319.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   115    318.9 MiB    318.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    319.5 MiB      0.6 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    319.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    318.9 MiB    318.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    319.6 MiB      0.7 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    318.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    319.6 MiB    319.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    319.8 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   125    319.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    319.8 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    319.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

    90    319.8 MiB    319.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    319.5 MiB    319.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    319.8 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    319.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   115    319.8 MiB    319.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    320.0 MiB      0.1 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

    90    320.0 MiB    320.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101

   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )

   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:

   133    319.8 MiB    319.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    320.0 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    320.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values

   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    320.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    319.8 MiB    319.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    320.1 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    319.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    320.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    320.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    320.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    320.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    320.2 MiB    320.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    320.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    320.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    320.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    320.4 MiB      0.2 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    320.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   376    320.8 MiB    320.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    320.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   115    317.8 MiB    317.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    318.3 MiB     -0.2 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    318.3 MiB      0.3 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    318.3 MiB     -0.1 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    323.0 MiB      4.7 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    318.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    323.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    318.5 MiB    318.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    323.0 MiB      4.5 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    318.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    323.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    323.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    339.9 MiB     16.9 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    339.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

    90    340.0 MiB    340.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    340.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    340.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    393.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    393.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    414.6 MiB     20.9 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    414.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    414.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    393.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   157                                         def find_nearest(array, value):
   158                                         
   159                                         	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   160                                         	# Returns the nearest value and nearest index from a given value and array.
   161                                         	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162                                         
   163    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       array = np.asarray(array)
   164    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       idx = (np.abs(array - value)).argmin()
   165                                         
   166    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       return array[idx], idx

    65    318.4 MiB    318.4 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    66                                         def get_kWe_kWt_over_mass_or_time(case, fluid, point, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i):
    67                                         
    68                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69                                             # For both H2O and sCO2, calculate:
    70                                             #       - kWe and kWt averages over mass flow rate & 
    71                                             #       - instantaneous kWe and kWt overtime 
    72                                             #
    73                                             # Outputs can be used for when interp_time == True, the following exists:
    74                                             #           sCO2_kWe_avg, sCO2_kWt_avg, H2O_kWe_avg, H2O_kWt_avg
    75                                             # Then, the following also get overriden when interp_time == True:
    76                                             #           sCO2_kWe, sCO2_kWt, H2O_kWe, H2O_kWt
    77                                             # 
    78                                             # When interp_time == False then all the output will exist and be plotted.
    79                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80                                         
    81                                             # need initialization here
    82    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWe_avg = None
    83    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWt_avg = None

    85    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWt_avg = None
    86    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       error_messages_d = {}
    87                                         
    88    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       if case == "utube":
    89                                         
    90                                         
    91    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "H2O" or fluid == "All":
    92    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWe_avg = u_H2O.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    93    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWt_avg = u_H2O.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    94                                         
    95    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "sCO2" or fluid == "All":
    96    318.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               sCO2_kWe_avg = u_sCO2.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]

    90    342.0 MiB    342.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    342.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    342.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.

   100                                                 """

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """

   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )

   115    342.0 MiB    342.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """

   125    345.8 MiB      3.8 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    345.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    345.9 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    345.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    345.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
    90    346.2 MiB    346.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
    90    346.2 MiB    346.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    342.0 MiB    342.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    345.9 MiB      4.0 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    342.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    345.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    345.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    346.2 MiB      0.3 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    346.2 MiB    346.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101

   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points

   91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98

   100                                                 """

   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
