   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    346.3 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    346.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    346.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    346.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    346.5 MiB      0.2 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    346.5 MiB    346.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96

   133    346.2 MiB    346.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    346.4 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    346.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    346.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    346.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    346.5 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208

   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":

   244                                                     # Tsurf=20, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=825, rho_m=2875, 
   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251                                         
   252                                                     times = times[14:]
   253                                                     Tout = Tout[14:]
   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]
   255

   376    346.5 MiB    346.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    346.9 MiB      0.4 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    346.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    346.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    347.0 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    347.0 MiB    347.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value == target:
   111    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    346.5 MiB    346.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    347.1 MiB      0.6 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    346.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    347.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    347.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    347.2 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   161                                             def interp_outlet_states(self, point, sbt_version, 
   162                                                             Tsurf, c_m, rho_m, radius_vertical, radius_lateral, n_laterals, lateral_flow, lateral_multiplier,
   163                                                             mesh, accuracy, mass_mode, temp_mode): # needs to be a callback option

   178                                                         )
   179                                                     )

   183    346.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   "all",
   184                                                     )  # unpacking point and adding "all" to the end of it. This tells interpolate_points to read in all of the time dimension
   185    346.5 MiB      0.3 MiB           1               Tout = self.interpolate_points(self.Tout, point_to_read_around, points)
   186    347.2 MiB      0.7 MiB           1               Pout = self.interpolate_points(self.Pout, point_to_read_around, points)
   187    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               times = self.time
   188                                                     # print("TEMP OUT: -----****")
   189                                                     # print(Tout)
   190                                         
   191                                                 else:
   192                                                     mdot, L2, L1, grad, D , Tinj, k = point
   193                                         
   194                                                     # print("\n -------------------------------- UI -------------------------------- ")
   195                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (m): {L2} L1 (m): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (K): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208                                         
   209                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "H20":
   210                                                         fluid = 1
   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":
   212                                                         fluid = 2
   213                                                     else:
   214                                                         fluid = 1 # water
   215                                                     
   216                                                     if mass_mode == "Constant":
   217                                                         mass_mode_b = 0
   218                                                     elif mass_mode == "Variable":
   219                                                         mass_mode_b = 1
   220                                         
   221                                                     if temp_mode == "Constant":
   222                                                         temp_mode_b = 0
   223                                                     elif temp_mode == "Variable":
   224                                                         temp_mode_b = 1
   225                                         
   226                                                     # print(f"sbt_version: {sbt_version} mesh_fineness: 0 clg_configuration: {case} fluid: {fluid}") ## uloop
   227                                         
   228                                                     times, Tout = run_sbt(
   229                                                     ## Model Specifications 
   230                                                     sbt_version=sbt_version, mesh_fineness=mesh, HYPERPARAM1=mass_mode_b, HYPERPARAM2="MassFlowRate.xlsx", 
   231                                                     HYPERPARAM3=temp_mode_b, HYPERPARAM4="InjectionTemperatures.xlsx", HYPERPARAM5=None, 
   232                                                     accuracy=accuracy,
   233                                         
   234                                                      ## Operations
   235                                                     clg_configuration=case, mdot=mdot, Tinj=Tinj, fluid=fluid, ## Operations
   236                                                     DrillingDepth_L1=L1, HorizontalExtent_L2=L2, #BoreholeDiameter=D, ## Wellbore Geometry
   237                                                     Diameter1=radius_vertical, Diameter2=radius_lateral, 
   238                                                     PipeParam3=n_laterals, PipeParam4=[lateral_flow], 
   239                                                     # PipeParam3=3, PipeParam4=[1/3,1/3,1/3], 
   240                                                     PipeParam5=lateral_multiplier, ## Tube Geometry
   241                                         
   242                                                     ## Geologic Properties
   243                                                     Tsurf=Tsurf, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=c_m, rho_m=rho_m, 
   244                                                     # Tsurf=20, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=825, rho_m=2875, 
   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251                                         
   252                                                     times = times[14:]
   253                                                     Tout = Tout[14:]
   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]
   255

   376    347.2 MiB    347.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    347.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   133    347.0 MiB    347.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    347.1 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    347.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    347.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    347.3 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    347.3 MiB    347.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98

   100                                                 """

   115    347.3 MiB    347.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    347.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    347.4 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    347.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    347.5 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    347.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    347.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   127                                                 ]

   129    347.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    347.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents

   133    347.3 MiB    347.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    347.5 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    347.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    347.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    347.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

   161                                             def interp_outlet_states(self, point, sbt_version, 
   162                                                             Tsurf, c_m, rho_m, radius_vertical, radius_lateral, n_laterals, lateral_flow, lateral_multiplier,
   163                                                             mesh, accuracy, mass_mode, temp_mode): # needs to be a callback option

   179                                                     )
   180

   183    347.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   "all",
   184                                                     )  # unpacking point and adding "all" to the end of it. This tells interpolate_points to read in all of the time dimension
   185    347.3 MiB      0.3 MiB           1               Tout = self.interpolate_points(self.Tout, point_to_read_around, points)
   186    347.6 MiB      0.3 MiB           1               Pout = self.interpolate_points(self.Pout, point_to_read_around, points)

   188                                                     # print("TEMP OUT: -----****")
   189                                                     # print(Tout)
   190                                         
   191                                                 else:
   192                                                     mdot, L2, L1, grad, D , Tinj, k = point
   193                                         
   194                                                     # print("\n -------------------------------- UI -------------------------------- ")
   195                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (m): {L2} L1 (m): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (K): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208                                         
   209                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "H20":
   210                                                         fluid = 1
   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":
   212                                                         fluid = 2
   213                                                     else:
   214                                                         fluid = 1 # water
   215                                                     
   216                                                     if mass_mode == "Constant":
   217                                                         mass_mode_b = 0
   218                                                     elif mass_mode == "Variable":
   219                                                         mass_mode_b = 1
   220                                         
   221                                                     if temp_mode == "Constant":
   222                                                         temp_mode_b = 0
   223                                                     elif temp_mode == "Variable":
   224                                                         temp_mode_b = 1
   225                                         
   226                                                     # print(f"sbt_version: {sbt_version} mesh_fineness: 0 clg_configuration: {case} fluid: {fluid}") ## uloop
   227                                         
   228                                                     times, Tout = run_sbt(
   229                                                     ## Model Specifications 
   230                                                     sbt_version=sbt_version, mesh_fineness=mesh, HYPERPARAM1=mass_mode_b, HYPERPARAM2="MassFlowRate.xlsx", 
   231                                                     HYPERPARAM3=temp_mode_b, HYPERPARAM4="InjectionTemperatures.xlsx", HYPERPARAM5=None, 
   232                                                     accuracy=accuracy,
   233

   235                                                     clg_configuration=case, mdot=mdot, Tinj=Tinj, fluid=fluid, ## Operations
   236                                                     DrillingDepth_L1=L1, HorizontalExtent_L2=L2, #BoreholeDiameter=D, ## Wellbore Geometry
   237                                                     Diameter1=radius_vertical, Diameter2=radius_lateral, 
   238                                                     PipeParam3=n_laterals, PipeParam4=[lateral_flow], 
   239                                                     # PipeParam3=3, PipeParam4=[1/3,1/3,1/3], 
   240                                                     PipeParam5=lateral_multiplier, ## Tube Geometry
   241                                         
   242                                                     ## Geologic Properties

   244                                                     # Tsurf=20, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=825, rho_m=2875, 
   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251

   253                                                     Tout = Tout[14:]
   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]
   255

   376    347.6 MiB    347.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    347.6 MiB    347.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    348.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    348.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    347.6 MiB    347.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    348.4 MiB      0.8 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    347.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    348.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    348.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    348.5 MiB      0.2 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    348.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   193

   195                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (m): {L2} L1 (m): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (K): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208

   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":
   212                                                         fluid = 2
   213                                                     else:
   214                                                         fluid = 1 # water
   215

   218                                                     elif mass_mode == "Variable":

   229                                                     ## Model Specifications

   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251

   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]

   376    348.6 MiB    348.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    348.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    348.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    348.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    348.8 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    348.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    350.0 MiB    350.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    350.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    350.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """

   108                                                     )

   112    350.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value > target:
   113    350.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   115    350.0 MiB    350.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """

   126    350.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]

   129    350.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    352.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    349.9 MiB    349.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    352.4 MiB      2.5 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    349.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    352.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    352.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    352.7 MiB      0.3 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   133    350.0 MiB    350.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    352.4 MiB      2.4 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    350.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    352.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    352.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    352.7 MiB      0.3 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   157                                         def find_nearest(array, value):

   402    352.7 MiB    352.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   403                                             def interp_kW_contour(self, param, point, Tout, Pout, Tamb=300.0):
   404                                         
   405    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = self.mdot
   406    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[4]
   407                                         
   408    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           accept_one_dim_only = False
   409                                         
   410    352.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           try:  # PropsSI accepts multidim for Python 3.10 only
   411                                         
   412    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   413                                                     enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   414                                                     entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   415                                                     entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   416                                         
   417    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           except Exception:  # PropsSI accepts only one dim for Python 3.8, 3.9. 3.11
   418                                         
   419    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               accept_one_dim_only = True
   420    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_one = Tout.shape[0]
   421    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_two = Tout.shape[1]
   422                                         
   423    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Tout = Tout.flatten(order="C")
   424    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Pout = Pout.flatten(order="C")
   425    352.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   426    352.9 MiB      0.2 MiB           1               enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   427    353.0 MiB      0.1 MiB           1               entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   428    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   429                                         
   430    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if not accept_one_dim_only:
   431                                         
   432                                                     kWe = (
   433                                                         mdot
   434                                                         * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   435                                                         / 1000.0
   436                                                     )
   437                                                     kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   438                                         
   439                                                 else:
   440                                         
   441    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (
   442    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in)
   443    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               ) / 1000.0
   444    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   445    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWe = mdot * calc
   446    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   447    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   448    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWt = mdot * calc
   449                                         
   450    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   411                                         
   412    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   413                                                     enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   414                                                     entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   415                                                     entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   416                                         
   417    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           except Exception:  # PropsSI accepts only one dim for Python 3.8, 3.9. 3.11
   418                                         
   419    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               accept_one_dim_only = True
   420    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_one = Tout.shape[0]
   421    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_two = Tout.shape[1]
   422                                         
   423    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Tout = Tout.flatten(order="C")
   424    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Pout = Pout.flatten(order="C")
   425    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   426    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   427    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   428    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   429                                         
   430    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if not accept_one_dim_only:
   431                                         
   432                                                     kWe = (
   433                                                         mdot
   434                                                         * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   435                                                         / 1000.0
   436                                                     )
   437                                                     kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   438                                         
   439                                                 else:
   440                                         
   441    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (
   442    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in)
   443    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               ) / 1000.0
   444    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   445    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWe = mdot * calc
   446    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   447    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   448    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWt = mdot * calc
   449                                         
   450    353.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    65    346.0 MiB    346.0 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    66                                         def get_kWe_kWt_over_mass_or_time(case, fluid, point, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i):
    67                                         
    68                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69                                             # For both H2O and sCO2, calculate:
    70                                             #       - kWe and kWt averages over mass flow rate & 
    71                                             #       - instantaneous kWe and kWt overtime 
    72                                             #
    73                                             # Outputs can be used for when interp_time == True, the following exists:
    74                                             #           sCO2_kWe_avg, sCO2_kWt_avg, H2O_kWe_avg, H2O_kWt_avg
    75                                             # Then, the following also get overriden when interp_time == True:
    76                                             #           sCO2_kWe, sCO2_kWt, H2O_kWe, H2O_kWt
    77                                             # 
    78                                             # When interp_time == False then all the output will exist and be plotted.
    79                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80                                         
    81                                             # need initialization here
    82    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWe_avg = None
    83    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWt_avg = None 
    84    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWe_avg = None
    85    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWt_avg = None
    86    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       error_messages_d = {}
    87                                         
    88    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       if case == "utube":
    89                                         
    90                                         
    91    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "H2O" or fluid == "All":
    92    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWe_avg = u_H2O.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    93    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWt_avg = u_H2O.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    94                                         
    95    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "sCO2" or fluid == "All":
    96    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               sCO2_kWe_avg = u_sCO2.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    97    346.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               sCO2_kWt_avg = u_sCO2.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]

    90    332.0 MiB    332.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    332.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    332.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.

   100                                                 """

   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points

   115    332.0 MiB    332.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    332.2 MiB     -0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    332.2 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    332.1 MiB     -0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    332.4 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    332.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    332.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    332.0 MiB    332.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    332.4 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    332.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    332.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    332.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    332.5 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    332.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points
