    20    343.0 MiB    343.0 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    21                                         def fixed_economic_inputs():
    22                                         
    23                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                             # Inputs that don't get changed by the app.
    25                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26                                         
    27    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       O_and_M_cost_plant = 0.015                 # Operation & maintance cost of surface plant expressed as fraction of total surface plant capital cost [-] (between 0 and 0.2)
    28    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       Pump_efficiency = 0.8                      # Pump efficiency for cirulcation pump (if required) [-] (between 0.5 and 1)
    29    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       Turbine_isentropic_efficiency = 0.9        # Isentropic efficiency for turbine when CO2 is working fluid [-] (between 0.8 and 1)
    30    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       Generator_efficiency = 0.98                # Conversion efficiency from mechanical turbine work to electricity [-] (between 0.8 and 1)
    31    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       Compressor_isentropic_efficiency = 0.9     # Isentropic efficiency for compressor when CO2 is working fluid [-] (between 0.8 and 1)
    32    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       T0 = 20 + to_kelvin_factor # to kelvin     # Dead-state temperature [K] (between 278.15 and 303.15)
    33    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       P0 = 1e5                                   # Dead-state pressure [Pa] (between 0.8e5 and 1.1e5)
    34    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       Electricity_rate = 0.1                     # Electricity rate in direct-use for pumping power (if pumping is required) [$/kWh] (between 0 and 0.5)
    35                                         
    36    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       return O_and_M_cost_plant, Pump_efficiency, Turbine_isentropic_efficiency, Generator_efficiency, Compressor_isentropic_efficiency, T0, P0, Electricity_rate

   402    337.7 MiB    337.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   403                                             def interp_kW_contour(self, param, point, Tout, Pout, Tamb=300.0):
   404                                         
   405    337.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = self.mdot
   406    337.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[4]
   407                                         
   408    337.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           accept_one_dim_only = False
   409                                         
   410    337.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           try:  # PropsSI accepts multidim for Python 3.10 only
   411                                         
   412    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   413                                                     enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   414                                                     entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   415                                                     entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   416                                         
   417    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           except Exception:  # PropsSI accepts only one dim for Python 3.8, 3.9. 3.11
   418                                         
   419    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               accept_one_dim_only = True
   420    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_one = Tout.shape[0]
   421    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               dim_two = Tout.shape[1]
   422                                         
   423    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Tout = Tout.flatten(order="C")
   424    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               Pout = Pout.flatten(order="C")
   425    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   426    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   427    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   428    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   429                                         
   430    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if not accept_one_dim_only:
   431                                         
   432                                                     kWe = (
   433                                                         mdot
   434                                                         * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   435                                                         / 1000.0
   436                                                     )
   437                                                     kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   438                                         
   439                                                 else:
   440                                         
   441    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (
   442    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in)
   443    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               ) / 1000.0
   444    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   445    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWe = mdot * calc
   446    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   447    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   448    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWt = mdot * calc
   449                                         
   450    337.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    340.8 MiB    340.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    340.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    340.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
    90    340.9 MiB    340.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    340.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    340.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    341.4 MiB    341.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    341.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    341.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )

   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.

   133    340.8 MiB    340.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    342.4 MiB      1.6 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    340.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    342.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    342.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    343.0 MiB      0.6 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    343.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    343.1 MiB    343.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    343.1 MiB    343.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           3           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value > target:
   113    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    343.3 MiB    343.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """

   115    343.1 MiB    343.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    343.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    343.3 MiB      0.2 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    343.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    343.4 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    343.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    343.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    343.1 MiB    343.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    343.4 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    343.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    343.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    343.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    343.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    343.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    343.5 MiB    343.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    343.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    343.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    344.0 MiB      0.5 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    344.1 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    344.2 MiB    344.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value > target:
   113    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   115    344.1 MiB    344.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    344.2 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    344.1 MiB    344.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    344.2 MiB      0.1 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    344.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    344.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    344.3 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    344.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    344.4 MiB    344.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101

   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    344.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    344.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    344.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    344.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    344.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    344.3 MiB    344.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    344.6 MiB      0.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    344.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   178                                                         )
   179                                                     )
   180

   184                                                     )  # unpacking point and adding "all" to the end of it. This tells interpolate_points to read in all of the time dimension

   193                                         
   194                                                     # print("\n -------------------------------- UI -------------------------------- ")
   195                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (m): {L2} L1 (m): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (K): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208                                         
   209                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "H20":
   210                                                         fluid = 1
   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":
   212                                                         fluid = 2
   213                                                     else:
   214                                                         fluid = 1 # water
   215                                                     
   216                                                     if mass_mode == "Constant":
   217                                                         mass_mode_b = 0
   218                                                     elif mass_mode == "Variable":
   219                                                         mass_mode_b = 1
   220                                         
   221                                                     if temp_mode == "Constant":

   223                                                     elif temp_mode == "Variable":
   224                                                         temp_mode_b = 1
   225                                         
   226                                                     # print(f"sbt_version: {sbt_version} mesh_fineness: 0 clg_configuration: {case} fluid: {fluid}") ## uloop
   227                                         
   228                                                     times, Tout = run_sbt(
   229                                                     ## Model Specifications

   232                                                     accuracy=accuracy,
   233

   236                                                     DrillingDepth_L1=L1, HorizontalExtent_L2=L2, #BoreholeDiameter=D, ## Wellbore Geometry
   237                                                     Diameter1=radius_vertical, Diameter2=radius_lateral, 
   238                                                     PipeParam3=n_laterals, PipeParam4=[lateral_flow], 
   239                                                     # PipeParam3=3, PipeParam4=[1/3,1/3,1/3], 
   240                                                     PipeParam5=lateral_multiplier, ## Tube Geometry
   241                                         
   242                                                     ## Geologic Properties

   244                                                     # Tsurf=20, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=825, rho_m=2875, 
   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251

   253                                                     Tout = Tout[14:]
   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]
   255

   376    344.6 MiB    344.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    344.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    344.7 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    344.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    347.8 MiB    347.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    347.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    347.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    348.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points

    90    356.0 MiB    356.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    356.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    356.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].

    65    355.8 MiB    355.8 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    66                                         def get_kWe_kWt_over_mass_or_time(case, fluid, point, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i):
    67                                         
    68                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69                                             # For both H2O and sCO2, calculate:
    70                                             #       - kWe and kWt averages over mass flow rate & 
    71                                             #       - instantaneous kWe and kWt overtime 
    72                                             #
    73                                             # Outputs can be used for when interp_time == True, the following exists:
    74                                             #           sCO2_kWe_avg, sCO2_kWt_avg, H2O_kWe_avg, H2O_kWt_avg
    75                                             # Then, the following also get overriden when interp_time == True:
    76                                             #           sCO2_kWe, sCO2_kWt, H2O_kWe, H2O_kWt
    77                                             # 
    78                                             # When interp_time == False then all the output will exist and be plotted.

    80

    85    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWt_avg = None
    86    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       error_messages_d = {}
    87                                         
    88    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       if case == "utube":
    89                                         
    90                                         
    91    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "H2O" or fluid == "All":
    92    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWe_avg = u_H2O.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    93    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWt_avg = u_H2O.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    94                                         
    95    355.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "sCO2" or fluid == "All":

   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.

   133    356.0 MiB    356.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    358.4 MiB      2.4 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    356.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    358.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    358.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    359.7 MiB      1.3 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    359.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    359.8 MiB    359.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98

    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   159                                         	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   160                                         	# Returns the nearest value and nearest index from a given value and array.
   161                                         	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162                                         
   163    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       array = np.asarray(array)
   164    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       idx = (np.abs(array - value)).argmin()
   165                                         
   166    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       return array[idx], idx

    76                                             #           sCO2_kWe, sCO2_kWt, H2O_kWe, H2O_kWt

    90    359.9 MiB    359.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    359.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    359.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    359.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):

   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    359.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value > target:
   113    359.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    360.4 MiB    360.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
   160                                         	# Returns the nearest value and nearest index from a given value and array.
   161                                         	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162                                         
   163    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       array = np.asarray(array)
   164    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       idx = (np.abs(array - value)).argmin()
   165                                         
   166    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       return array[idx], idx

    90    360.5 MiB    360.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           3           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               if value > target:
   113    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    65    360.0 MiB    360.0 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    66                                         def get_kWe_kWt_over_mass_or_time(case, fluid, point, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i):
    67                                         
    68                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69                                             # For both H2O and sCO2, calculate:
    70                                             #       - kWe and kWt averages over mass flow rate & 
    71                                             #       - instantaneous kWe and kWt overtime 
    72                                             #
    73                                             # Outputs can be used for when interp_time == True, the following exists:
    74                                             #           sCO2_kWe_avg, sCO2_kWt_avg, H2O_kWe_avg, H2O_kWt_avg
    75                                             # Then, the following also get overriden when interp_time == True:
    76                                             #           sCO2_kWe, sCO2_kWt, H2O_kWe, H2O_kWt
    77                                             # 
    78                                             # When interp_time == False then all the output will exist and be plotted.
    79                                             # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80                                         
    81                                             # need initialization here
    82    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWe_avg = None
    83    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_kWt_avg = None 
    84    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWe_avg = None
    85    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       H2O_kWt_avg = None
    86    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       error_messages_d = {}
    87                                         
    88    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       if case == "utube":
    89                                         
    90                                         
    91    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "H2O" or fluid == "All":
    92    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWe_avg = u_H2O.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    93    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               H2O_kWt_avg = u_H2O.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    94                                         
    95    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if fluid == "sCO2" or fluid == "All":
    96    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               sCO2_kWe_avg = u_sCO2.kWe_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    97    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               sCO2_kWt_avg = u_sCO2.kWt_avg[:, arg_L2_i, arg_L1_i, arg_grad_i, arg_D_i, arg_Tinj_i, arg_k_i]
    98

   100

   105

    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """

   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )

   115    359.8 MiB    359.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """

   125    360.1 MiB      0.1 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)

   129    360.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents

   133    359.8 MiB    359.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    360.5 MiB      0.8 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    361.1 MiB      0.6 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    361.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    361.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)


Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py
