   133    359.8 MiB    359.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    360.6 MiB      0.9 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    360.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    360.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    361.3 MiB      0.6 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    361.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    361.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    361.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(

   376    361.3 MiB    361.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    361.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    361.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    361.7 MiB      0.4 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    361.7 MiB    361.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    361.8 MiB    361.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    360.2 MiB    360.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    361.8 MiB      1.6 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    360.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    361.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    361.9 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    361.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   115    361.1 MiB    361.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,

   123                                                 """

   127                                                 ]

   129    361.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

    90    362.1 MiB    362.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value == target:
   111    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    362.2 MiB    362.2 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    362.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    361.1 MiB    361.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    362.1 MiB      0.9 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    361.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    362.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    362.4 MiB      0.3 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    362.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    362.4 MiB    362.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    362.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    362.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   115    361.8 MiB    361.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """

   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

    90    362.5 MiB    362.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    362.9 MiB    362.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          10           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          10               if value == target:
   111    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   133    361.7 MiB    361.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    362.5 MiB      0.7 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    361.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    362.9 MiB      0.4 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    362.9 MiB    362.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt


    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101

Filename: /Users/bern700/git_repositories/GEO/public/GeoCLUSTER/dash_app/clgs.py

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
    90    362.9 MiB    362.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """

   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points

    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value == target:
   111    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   119                                                 """

   133    362.5 MiB    362.5 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    362.9 MiB      0.4 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    362.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    363.1 MiB      0.2 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    363.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   376    363.1 MiB    363.1 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    363.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    363.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    363.2 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    363.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   133    362.6 MiB    362.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    362.9 MiB      0.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    362.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    363.3 MiB      0.4 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    363.3 MiB    363.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           6           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           6               if value == target:
   111    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    363.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           5               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

   376    363.6 MiB    363.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    363.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    363.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    363.7 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

    90    363.7 MiB    363.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value > target:
   113    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
   133    362.9 MiB    362.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    363.5 MiB      0.6 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    362.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    363.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    363.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    363.7 MiB      0.2 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:

   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)

   115    363.3 MiB    363.3 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    363.7 MiB      0.4 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    364.2 MiB      0.5 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    364.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   162                                                             Tsurf, c_m, rho_m, radius_vertical, radius_lateral, n_laterals, lateral_flow, lateral_multiplier,
   163                                                             mesh, accuracy, mass_mode, temp_mode): # needs to be a callback option

   184                                                     )  # unpacking point and adding "all" to the end of it. This tells interpolate_points to read in all of the time dimension

   186    364.3 MiB      1.0 MiB           1               Pout = self.interpolate_points(self.Pout, point_to_read_around, points)

   188                                                     # print("TEMP OUT: -----****")
   189                                                     # print(Tout)
   190                                         
   191                                                 else:
   192                                                     mdot, L2, L1, grad, D , Tinj, k = point
   193                                         
   194                                                     # print("\n -------------------------------- UI -------------------------------- ")
   195                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (m): {L2} L1 (m): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (K): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   196                                                     
   197                                                     # AB UNIT CONVERSIONS AND RENAMING
   198                                                     # DIAMETER NEEDS TO GO FROM M TO KM so divide by 1000
   199                                                     L2 = L2/1000
   200                                                     L1 = L1/1000
   201                                                     Tinj = Tinj-273.15
   202                                                     # print(f"mdot (kg/s): {mdot} L2 (km): {L2} L1 (km): {L1} GeoGrad (K/m): {grad} BoreDiam (m): {D} Tinj (C): {Tinj} RockThermCond, k ((W/m-K)): {k}")
   203                                         
   204                                                     if self.case == "coaxial":
   205                                                         case = 1
   206                                                     if self.case == "utube":
   207                                                         case = 2
   208                                         
   209                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "H20":
   210                                                         fluid = 1
   211                                                     if self.CP_fluid == "CO2":
   212                                                         fluid = 2
   213                                                     else:
   214                                                         fluid = 1 # water
   215                                                     
   216                                                     if mass_mode == "Constant":
   217                                                         mass_mode_b = 0
   218                                                     elif mass_mode == "Variable":
   219                                                         mass_mode_b = 1
   220                                         
   221                                                     if temp_mode == "Constant":
   222                                                         temp_mode_b = 0
   223                                                     elif temp_mode == "Variable":
   224                                                         temp_mode_b = 1
   225                                         
   226                                                     # print(f"sbt_version: {sbt_version} mesh_fineness: 0 clg_configuration: {case} fluid: {fluid}") ## uloop
   227                                         
   228                                                     times, Tout = run_sbt(
   229                                                     ## Model Specifications 
   230                                                     sbt_version=sbt_version, mesh_fineness=mesh, HYPERPARAM1=mass_mode_b, HYPERPARAM2="MassFlowRate.xlsx",

   232                                                     accuracy=accuracy,

   236                                                     DrillingDepth_L1=L1, HorizontalExtent_L2=L2, #BoreholeDiameter=D, ## Wellbore Geometry
   237                                                     Diameter1=radius_vertical, Diameter2=radius_lateral, 
   238                                                     PipeParam3=n_laterals, PipeParam4=[lateral_flow], 
   239                                                     # PipeParam3=3, PipeParam4=[1/3,1/3,1/3], 
   240                                                     PipeParam5=lateral_multiplier, ## Tube Geometry
   241                                         
   242                                                     ## Geologic Properties
   243                                                     Tsurf=Tsurf, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=c_m, rho_m=rho_m, 
   244                                                     # Tsurf=20, GeoGradient=grad, k_m=k, c_m=825, rho_m=2875, 
   245                                                     )
   246                                         
   247                                                     # self.time = times
   248                                         
   249                                                     constant_pressure = 2e7 # 200 Bar in pascal || 2.09e7 
   250                                                     Pout = constant_pressure * np.ones_like(Tout)
   251                                         
   252                                                     times = times[14:]
   253                                                     Tout = Tout[14:]
   254                                                     Pout = Pout[14:]
   255

   376    364.4 MiB    364.4 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   377                                             def interp_kW(
   378                                                 self,
   379                                                 point,
   380                                                 Tout,
   381                                                 Pout,
   382                                                 Tamb=300.0, # AB: is this surface temperature? What is Tamb?
   383                                             ):
   384                                         
   385    364.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           mdot = point[0]
   386    364.4 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           Tinj = point[5]
   387                                         
   388    364.5 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_out = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   389    364.6 MiB      0.1 MiB           1           enthalpy_in = CP.PropsSI("H", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   390    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_out = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tout, "P", Pout, self.CP_fluid)
   391    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   392                                         
   393    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWe = (
   394    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           3               mdot
   395    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   396    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               / 1000.0
   397                                                 )
   398    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   399                                         
   400    364.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   115    363.7 MiB    363.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.
   121                                                 If "all" is passed in as any of the coordinates of the point,
   122                                                 then it'll slice across the entirety of that coordinate's dimension
   123                                                 """
   124    364.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           indices = [
   125    364.3 MiB      0.6 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    364.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]
   128    364.9 MiB      0.6 MiB           2           data = zarr_array[
   129    364.3 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    364.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   133    363.7 MiB    363.7 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    364.9 MiB      1.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    363.7 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    364.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    364.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    365.8 MiB      0.9 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    365.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   133    355.9 MiB    355.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    367.0 MiB     11.1 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    355.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    367.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    367.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    367.6 MiB      0.6 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    367.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

    90    367.6 MiB    367.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    367.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    367.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    367.8 MiB    367.8 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           2           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           2               if value == target:
   111    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    367.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

Line #    Mem usage    Increment  Occurrences   Line Contents
=============================================================
   133    360.0 MiB    360.0 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    367.2 MiB      7.1 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    360.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    367.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    367.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    367.9 MiB      0.7 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   135                                                 """

   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    367.1 MiB      7.3 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    359.8 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    367.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    367.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    367.9 MiB      0.8 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)

    90    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105                                                 if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109                                                 for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110                                                     if value == target:
   111                                                         return slice(i, i + 1)
   112                                                     if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)
