    90    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points

   105    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:

        Construct a new Font object

        Sets this axis' title font. Note that the title's font used to
        be customized by the now deprecated `titlefont` attribute.

        Parameters
        ----------
        arg
            dict of properties compatible with this constructor or
            an instance of
            :class:`plotly.graph_objs.layout.xaxis.title.Font`
        color

        family
            HTML font family - the typeface that will be applied by
            the web browser. The web browser will only be able to
            apply a font if it is available on the system which it
            operates. Provide multiple font families, separated by
            commas, to indicate the preference in which to apply
            fonts if they aren't available on the system. The Chart
            Studio Cloud (at https://chart-studio.plotly.com or on-
            premise) generates images on a server, where only a
            select number of fonts are installed and supported.
            These include "Arial", "Balto", "Courier New", "Droid
            Sans",, "Droid Serif", "Droid Sans Mono", "Gravitas
            One", "Old Standard TT", "Open Sans", "Overpass", "PT
            Sans Narrow", "Raleway", "Times New Roman".
        size


        Returns
        -------
        Font

        :param path: URL path being requested.
        :param base_url: Base URL where the app is being served, which
            ``path`` is relative to. If not given, built from
            :data:`PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME`, ``subdomain``,
            :data:`SERVER_NAME`, and :data:`APPLICATION_ROOT`.
        :param subdomain: Subdomain name to append to
            :data:`SERVER_NAME`.
        :param url_scheme: Scheme to use instead of
            :data:`PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME`.
        :param data: The request body, either as a string or a dict of
            form keys and values.
        :param json: If given, this is serialized as JSON and passed as
            ``data``. Also defaults ``content_type`` to
            ``application/json``.
        :param args: other positional arguments passed to
            :class:`~werkzeug.test.EnvironBuilder`.
        :param kwargs: other keyword arguments passed to
            :class:`~werkzeug.test.EnvironBuilder`.
        """
        from .testing import EnvironBuilder

        builder = EnvironBuilder(self, *args, **kwargs)

        try:
            return self.request_context(builder.get_environ())
        finally:
            builder.close()

    def wsgi_app(
        self, environ: WSGIEnvironment, start_response: StartResponse
    ) -> cabc.Iterable[bytes]:
        """The actual WSGI application. This is not implemented in
        :meth:`__call__` so that middlewares can be applied without
        losing a reference to the app object. Instead of doing this::

            app = MyMiddleware(app)

        It's a better idea to do this instead::

            app.wsgi_app = MyMiddleware(app.wsgi_app)

        Then you still have the original application object around and
        can continue to call methods on it.

        .. versionchanged:: 0.7
            Teardown events for the request and app contexts are called
            even if an unhandled error occurs. Other events may not be
            called depending on when an error occurs during dispatch.
            See :ref:`callbacks-and-errors`.

        :param environ: A WSGI environment.
        :param start_response: A callable accepting a status code,
            a list of headers, and an optional exception context to
            start the response.
        """
        ctx = self.request_context(environ)
        error: BaseException | None = None
        try:
            try:
                ctx.push()
                response = self.full_dispatch_request()
            except Exception as e:
                error = e
                response = self.handle_exception(e)
           102    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"

   112    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          59               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

    90    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
    91                                             def get_parameter_indices(self, array, target):
    92                                                 """
    93                                                 returns the slice that would give you the points in the given array around target. Assumes that array is sorted.
    94                                                 I.E if array is [1, 2, 3] and you give it 1.5, the points around 1.5 are [1, 2], so it will return the indices of [1,2], which are [0, 1].
    95                                                 However, since we need to use this indices to slice into the 8D matrix, we return a slice object that will slice indices.
    96                                         
    97                                                 if the array contains the target exactly, it'll just return a slice for getting specifically that point.
    98                                         
    99                                                 If we pass in "all" as the target, it will return a slice that will slice the entirety of that array
   100                                                 """
   101                                         
   102    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target == "all":
   103                                                     return slice(None)  # slice all of the points
   104                                                 # NOTE: PROBLEM CLAUSE FOR NOT ALLOWING GEOGRAD TO BE MORE THAN 0.7
   105    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if target < array[0] or target > array[-1]:
   106                                                     raise Exception(
   107                                                         f"expected given value {target} to be between min and max of given array ({array[0], array[-1]})"
   108                                                     )
   109    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          60           for i, value in enumerate(array):
   110    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          60               if value == target:
   111    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   return slice(i, i + 1)
   112    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB          59               if value > target:
   113                                                         return slice(i - 1, i + 1)

z|␀␀␀Ćß£0␀␀␀└±£0␀␀␀␀(w|␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀03"6␀␀␀░7"6␀␀␀p2"6␀␀␀p8"6␀␀␀P1"6␀␀␀09"6␀␀␀É0"6␀␀␀ą"6␀␀␀0Å:4␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀@─y|␀␀␀@▐||␀␀␀└░£0␀␀␀@ģ/␀␀␀@z|␀␀␀@z|␀␀␀└±£0␀␀␀␀Ø0␀␀␀ 0w|␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀┤y|␀␀␀␀╬||␀␀␀ĆĀ£0␀␀␀␀␀ģ/␀␀␀␀°y|␀␀␀␀

   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values
   118                                             ):
   119                                                 """
   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.

   123                                                 """

   125    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           8               self.get_parameter_indices(params, value)
   126    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               for value, params in zip(point, parameter_values)
   127                                                 ]

   129    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               tuple(indices)
   130                                                 ]  # need to convert into into a tuple or zarr thinks we're doing fancy indexing
   131    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return indices, data

   115    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   116                                             def read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   117                                                 self, zarr_array, point, parameter_values

   120                                                 reads the values around the given point(s) for interpolation from the passed in zarr_array.

   133    367.6 MiB    367.6 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    370.1 MiB      2.5 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    367.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    475.5 MiB    105.4 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    475.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   113    360.5 MiB    360.5 MiB           1   @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   114                                         def generate_subsurface_lineplots(interp_time, fluid, case, arg_mdot, arg_L2, arg_L1, arg_grad, arg_D, arg_Tinj, arg_k, scale, model,
   115                                                     Tsurf, c_m, rho_m, radius_vertical, radius_lateral, n_laterals, lateral_flow, lateral_multiplier,
   116                                                     mesh, accuracy, mass_mode, temp_mode):
   117                                         
   118                                             # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   119                                             # Creates Plotly with 5 subplots:
   120                                             #
   121                                             #       kWe vs. mass flow rate
   122                                             #       kWt vs. mass flow rate
   123                                             #       Temperature vs. Time (years)
   124                                             #       Pressure vs. Time (years)
   125                                             #       kWe vs. time
   126                                             #
   127                                             # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128                                         
   129    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       sCO2_Tout = sCO2_Pout = H2O_Tout = H2O_Pout = sCO2_kWe = sCO2_kWt = H2O_kWe = H2O_kWt = None
   130                                         
   131    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1       if model == "HDF5":
   132    360.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           sbt_version = 0
   133                                             elif model == "SBT V1.0":
   134                                                 sbt_version = 1
   135                                             else:

   133    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    370.1 MiB      2.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    459.9 MiB     89.8 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    459.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   133    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    370.1 MiB      2.2 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    370.1 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    461.0 MiB     90.8 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    461.0 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points

   428    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               entropy_in = CP.PropsSI("S", "T", Tinj, "P", self.Pinj, self.CP_fluid)
   429                                         
   430    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           if not accept_one_dim_only:
   431                                         
   432                                                     kWe = (
   433                                                         mdot
   434                                                         * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in))
   435                                                         / 1000.0
   436                                                     )
   437                                                     kWt = mdot * (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   438                                         
   439                                                 else:
   440                                         
   441    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (
   442    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1                   enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in - Tamb * (entropy_out - entropy_in)
   443    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               ) / 1000.0
   444    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   445    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWe = mdot * calc
   446    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = (enthalpy_out - enthalpy_in) / 1000.0
   447    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               calc = np.reshape(calc, (dim_one, dim_two), order="C")
   448    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               kWt = mdot * calc
   449                                         
   450    457.2 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return kWe, kWt

   133    367.9 MiB    367.9 MiB           1       @profile(stream=memory_profile_file)
   134                                             def interpolate_points(self, zarr_array, point_to_read_around, points):
   135                                                 """
   136                                                 Reads in only the data directly around points_to_read_around from zarr_array and interpolates the passed in points on that data.
   137                                                 Essentially a wrapper around interpn where we only read the data we need.
   138                                                 Alot of the data from the original zarr_array is not needed for interpolation since the default linear interpolation method of interpn only needs the corners of the hypercube around a given point to interpolate it's value
   139                                                 Instead of the entire 8D parameter space.
   140                                                 """
   141    391.6 MiB     23.7 MiB           2           indices, values_around_point = self.read_values_around_point_for_interpolation(
   142    367.9 MiB      0.0 MiB           1               zarr_array, point_to_read_around, self.ivars
   143                                                 )
   144    391.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9           grid = [
   145    391.6 MiB      0.0 MiB           9               params[these_indices] for these_indices, params in zip(indices, self.ivars)
   146                                                 ]  # the grid is the values of the parameters at the points we're interpolating between
   147    457.5 MiB     65.9 MiB           1           interpolated_points = interpn(grid, values_around_point, points)
   148    457.5 MiB      0.0 MiB           1           return interpolated_points
